channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How Incel Slang Has Spread to Mainstream Media and Government
item.description: In recent months, the use of 'incel' and 'looksmaxxing' slang has become more common, even in official government accounts. A tweet from the US Department of Defense boasting about military capabiliti...

item.title: UK retailers lock chocolate in anti-theft boxes due to rising theft
item.description: Retailers across the UK are increasingly securing chocolate bars in anti-theft boxes due to a surge in organised shoplifting. High-value chocolate, such as Cadbury Dairy Milk and Creme Eggs, has becom...

item.title: 16-year-old convicted for membership in banned neo-Nazi organisation
item.description: A 16-year-old boy from Northumberland has been found guilty of being a member of the banned extremist group The Base and of possessing and sharing terrorist publications. Leeds Crown Court delivered a...

item.title: How Structural Barriers Push Streaming Users Toward Piracy in MENA
item.description: In the Middle East and North Africa (MENA), access to streaming platforms remains uneven due to economic, political, and technological barriers. Countries like Syria and Lebanon face significant issue...

item.title: France Seeks to Restrict US Envoy Charles Kushner’s Access After Dispute Over Extremism Comments
item.description: A diplomatic dispute has emerged between France and the United States after comments posted by the US embassy in Paris sparked anger within the French government. The controversy began following the d...

item.title: Former Labour MP Jonathan Ashworth reveals stroke on New Year's Day caused by high blood pressure, urges blood pressure checks
item.description: Former Labour MP Jonathan Ashworth, who represented Leicester South from 2011 to 2024, has disclosed that he suffered an ischaemic stroke on New Year's Day 2026. The 47-year-old experienced stiffness ...

item.title: UK Parliament to Debate Release of Documents on Prince Andrew's Trade Envoy Role
item.description: The Liberal Democrats plan to push for the release of documents concerning Prince Andrew's appointment as a UK trade envoy, following his recent arrest. The party intends to table a motion in the Hous...

item.title: US-Israeli Strikes on Iran: A Breach of International Law and Diplomacy
item.description: The joint US-Israeli strikes on Iran in February 2025 are seen as a significant violation of international law. These attacks, launched while negotiations between the US and Iran were still active, ha...

item.title: England's Batting Woes Against Spin Threaten World Cup Progress
item.description: England's batting lineup continues to struggle against spin bowling in the ongoing T20 World Cup. Despite a 51-run victory over Sri Lanka, four of the top five England batters were dismissed by spinne...

item.title: UK Celebrates First Birth from Deceased Donor Womb Transplant
item.description: A baby boy named Hugo has become the first child in the UK born using a womb transplanted from a deceased donor. His mother, Grace Bell, in her 30s and born without a viable womb due to MRKH syndrome,...

item.title: Brit Awards 2026 Highlights: Olivia Dean's Big Wins, Manchester Move, and Key Performances and Tributes
item.description: The 2026 Brit Awards, hosted by Jack Whitehall and held for the first time in Manchester at the Co-op Live arena, celebrated British and international music with standout moments beyond the awards the...

item.title: UK Defence Secretary Provides Update on Middle East Operations Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: The UK has revealed details of its operations in the Middle East following rising tensions with Iran. Defence Secretary John Healey confirmed that the UK is not involved in strikes on Iran but is focu...

item.title: Eric Paré's Stunning Light Painting Photography Featuring Kim Henry
item.description: Light painting is a technique that has been around for decades, but photographer Eric Paré has taken it to new heights with his breathtaking images. Using long-exposure photography and models, Paré cr...

item.title: Trump Warns of Higher Tariffs on Nations Reneging on Recent Trade Agreements After Supreme Court Ruling
item.description: US President Donald Trump has issued a strong warning on Truth Social, threatening significantly higher tariffs on countries that attempt to back away from or renegotiate recent trade deals following ...

item.title: Scottish family in Dubai hears anti-missile defences during Iran retaliation
item.description: A Scottish man living in Dubai, Gary Goldie, described the concern his children had when they heard the sounds of anti-missile defences intercepting missiles launched from Iran. He explained that whil...

item.title: Mother of Terminally Ill Teen Supports Assisted Dying Debate as Senedd Prepares to Vote
item.description: The mother of a 19-year-old terminally ill teenager has said she now supports changes to UK law that would allow assisted dying, after witnessing her son’s prolonged suffering from an inoperable brain...

item.title: UK Police Forces Continue Using Microsoft Copilot After AI Error in Football Fan Ban Case
item.description: At least 21 police forces across England continue to use Microsoft’s Copilot AI tool despite a major controversy involving West Midlands Police (WMP), which blocked access to the software after it gen...

item.title: Italian Cricket Federation Faces Governance Turmoil Following Sexual Assault Allegation Against Senior Official
item.description: Italian cricket is facing significant turmoil following the emergence of a sexual assault allegation against Prabath Ekneligoda, the women’s cricket coordinator of the Federazione Cricket Italiana (FC...

item.title: Authorities Investigate Canine Distemper Outbreak Killing 72 Tigers at Chiang Mai Park
item.description: Authorities in Chiang Mai, northern Thailand, are investigating the deaths of 72 tigers at Tiger Kingdom Chiang Mai, a popular tourist attraction where visitors can interact with the animals. The deat...

item.title: Labour's Struggles and the End of Traditional Politics
item.description: Following Labour's third-place finish in the Gorton and Denton by-election, Keir Starmer’s response lacked the self-reflection many had hoped for. Instead of acknowledging Labour’s failure to connect ...
